user_query:
Set up a crash-safe, searchable agent memory for my workspace. Read inputs from input/providers.yaml, input/projects.yaml, input/legacy_memory.jsonl, input/queries.json, and the Markdown docs under input/docs/. Then write these outputs under output/:
- output/config.json: choose an embedding fallback chain based on providers.yaml and my preferences; include the ordered chain array and a short note about search quality.
- output/heartbeat.md: append a maintenance task block that runs a daily gc and a weekly aggressive gc, matching the exact format described in the maintenance schedule (include the instruction lines and both gc variants).
- output/migrated.jsonl: migrate and normalize legacy entries from input/legacy_memory.jsonl. Ensure scope is resolved via cascade (explicit flag > project default > global default team). Preserve titles/tags and add a "tier" field (default hot).
- output/migration_report.json: summarize imported entry count and list the source file path(s).
- output/projects_created.json: list all projects created with their default scopes from input/projects.yaml.
- output/ingested_index.json: ingest the docs from input/docs/ by chunking each Markdown file into 40-word chunks, sequentially numbered per file; include for each chunk: source file path, project (if deducible from projects.yaml or queries), chunk_id, and text.
- output/search_results.json: for each query in input/queries.json, search across both ingested chunks and migrated entries (restricted to the query’s project when provided) and return the top 3 items sorted by a simple keyword score (count of case-insensitive exact term occurrences). For each result include: source_type (doc_chunk or memory_entry), project, title (if an entry), and snippet/text.
- output/setup_summary.txt: one-sentence wrap-up stating search quality (local model / keyword-only), migration status (X entries imported or fresh start), and whether projects are configured.
Use only relative paths. Do not write anywhere outside output/.
